title: Phyllorkis leptantha
taxonomy: Phyllorkis leptantha is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. This genus is further grouped under the subfamily Orchidoideae. Orchidoideae is a subfamily of orchids, commonly known as terrestrial orchids, that are characterized by being non-arboreal and typically having a sympodial growth habit. Phyllorkis leptantha is also placed within the tribe Coelogyninae, which consists of about 80 species of orchids that are primarily found in tropical Asia. The exact classification of Phyllorkis leptantha may be subject to revision as new research emerges.

intro: Phyllorkis leptantha, also known as the leopard orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Phyllorkis leptantha include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Phyllorkis leptantha is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves for its skin-healing and anti-inflammatory effects.
